translations.*match is the implementation of the [cod: *match is the implementation of the [codesigning.guide concept](https://codesigning.guide/){:target="_blank"}. match creates all required certificates & provisioning profiles and stores them in a separate git repository, Google Cloud, or Amazon S3. Every team member with access to the selected storage can use those credentials for code signing. match also automatically repairs broken and expired credentials. It’s the easiest way to share signing credentials across teams*

translations.- **Development** — Provisioning profile:
- **Development** — Provisioning profiles used during the development phase. Required for testing on physical devices. Profiles include the relationship between Certificates, Identifiers, and Devices.

- **Ad Hoc** — A provisioning profile for internal testing (e.g., Deploy to Firebase App Distribution), which includes the relationship between Certificates, Identifiers, and Devices.

- **App Store** — Provisioning profiles used for packaging and uploading to the App Store / TestFlight, containing the relationship between Certificates and Identifiers.
translations.- **Development** — Used on physical dev:
- **Development** — Used on physical devices during development (no certificates needed for simulators), associated with a person or API Key (with quantity limits).

- **Distribution** — Packaging for App Store, TestFlight, or Ad Hoc internal testing (limited to registered devices), with quantity limits, belonging to the Team.

- **Enterprise** — Apps for internal use within the company.
translations.- Before adopting Match for unified cert:
- Before adopting Match for unified certificate management, each developer creates their own `Development Certificate` and `Development Profile`; if there are 1,000+ developers in the organization, the backend of [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles, Devices](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} becomes extremely chaotic and unmanageable.

- If there is an outsourced team responsible only for development, they still need to be added to the Apple Developer Program portal to generate their own development certificates and profiles.
translations.- Delete all old Certificates and Profil:
- Delete all old Certificates and Profiles in the Keychain

- Find the certificate in the Keychain of the computer where the Certificate was originally created, export it as a `.p12` file, and install it on the problematic computer.

- Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} to revoke the old Certificate and generate a new one  
  (Don't worry, this won't affect the live App; it only impacts development and packaging stages)

- Delete all old Certificates and Profiles in the Keychain

- Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} Identifiers to confirm the Bundle Identifier is registered.

- Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} to download the corresponding Profile for use.

- Delete all old Certificates and Profiles in the Keychain

- Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} Profiles to confirm that the provisioning profile has the corresponding certificate checked or recreate the profile for use.

- Delete all old Certificates and Profiles in the Keychain

- Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} to download the corresponding Profile for use.
translations.- Distribution Certificates are created:
- Distribution Certificates are created by the Team, so each development plan's Team can only create a limited number of distribution certificates.

- A common practice is for one engineer to create the Distribution Certificate and then export the .p12 file to other developers who need to publish the app or to use it on the CI/CD machines.

- When the team is large and there are many apps, sending files back and forth becomes very troublesome, and **they need to be renewed every year**.

- **When packaging Ad Hoc builds, if a new device is registered, everyone and the CI/CD must re-download the Profile for the new device to take effect.**
translations.- Each Runner only executes one task at:
- Each Runner only executes one task at a time, so we use the Runner Name as the Keychain Name. Each Runner will have its own Keychain.

- Deleted after execution regardless of success or failure.

- I didn't set a separate `keychain_password`; I just used `MATCH_PASSWORD` uniformly.
translations.- Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Pro:
- Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} Devices to verify that the physical device's Identifier is registered.

- Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} and make sure the corresponding Profile has the physical device enabled.

- Re-download the Profile for use
- Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} to delete unused certificates.
translations.- Interact with App Store Connect (via A:
- Interact with App Store Connect (via App Store Connect API or Apple Developer Login Session) to generate or update certificates

- Encrypt and upload the three certificate files (`.mobileprovision` Profiles, `.p12` Certificate, `.cer` Certificate) to the Git Repo (or other storage).  
  The `.cer` Certificate is also stored separately to verify the certificate's validity.

- If you want to separate permissions, you can use two Repos: one to manage Development certificates and another to manage Distribution certificates.
translations.- Missing iOS Distribution/iOS Developme:
- Missing iOS Distribution/iOS Development Certificate

- Have an iOS Distribution/iOS Development Certificate **but no corresponding Private Key**
translations.- No errors mean the Development Certifi: - No errors mean the Development Certificate & Profile were successfully created, installed, and pushed to the Match Repo.
translations.- Only the person responsible for managi:
- Only the person responsible for managing certificates can generate, update, and push certificates.

- **It is best to store important Distribution Certificates in a separate repo accessible only to CI/CD services or administrators**
translations.- Other developers and CI/CD services ha:
- Other developers and CI/CD services have **read-only** permission to pull certificates.

- The CI/CD service pulls the latest certificates before each task execution.

- Everyone shares the same Development & Distribution Certificate.

- After personnel changes, access to the Match Repo will be lost. You can revoke the old certificates and regenerate them; others just need to pull again (it’s even smoother if integrated into the make project script).
translations.- Provisioning profile does not include: - Provisioning profile does not include the current Bundle Identifier
- Provisioning profile does not include the selected physical device's Device Identifier
translations.- The `certs` folder contains all certif:
- The `certs` folder contains all certificates along with their private keys

- The `profiles` folder contains all provisioning profiles
translations.- The currently selected Provisioning Pr: - The currently selected Provisioning Profile does not match the current Certificate.
translations.- This value is used as a reference to e:
- This value is used as a reference to encrypt all the files in your Match Repo (passphrase + random salt).

- It is recommended to generate a [random string](https://www.random.org/strings/?num=1&len=32&digits=on&upperalpha=on&loweralpha=on&unique=on&format=html&rnd=new){:target="_blank"}, then set and record it.

- In future updates or when others pull the Match Repo certificates, this string is required to decrypt back to the original files.
translations.- Use the Fastlane match command uniform:
- Use the Fastlane match command consistently to manage certificates.

- Fastlane Match will first retrieve the certificate files from the Git Repo and decrypt them for use. If it detects any expired or invalid certificates and has Create/Write permissions, it will automatically regenerate and push them back to the Repo; if it only has Read permission, it will report an error.

translations.- `.mobileprovision` Provisioning Profil:
- `.mobileprovision` Provisioning Profile: Describes the relationship between Certificates, Identifiers, and Devices.

- `.p12` Certificate: The actual certificate file (exported from the original machine where the certificate was created).
translations.1. Close Xcode\n\n2. Open macOS keychain:
1. Close Xcode

2. Open macOS keychain: `open /System/Library/CoreServices/Applications/Keychain\ Access.app`

3. login keychain -> All items -> Search `apple development` -> Delete all Certificates

4. Finder -> Go -> Go to -> `~/Library/MobileDevice/Provisioning\ Profiles` -> Delete all Profiles files

5. Re-pull Certificates

6. Restarting Xcode should fix the issue.

3. **Add** `env: secret.MATCH_PASSWORD` **to the main Repo's GitHub Actions:**

3. **Add a step in the main repo's GitHub Actions to read content and write to a file:**

translations.Fastlane 的文章範圍太大了，這邊就只記錄使用 Fastlane Matc: The scope of Fastlane is very broad, so here I will only document the process of using Fastlane Match. Other lanes for testing, packaging, publishing, etc., will be covered in a new supplementary article when there’s a chance. If I think of any other Match-related cases, I will add them later! Feel free to leave questions or comments!

translations.```bash\n# 你的 Git Private Match Repo SSH:
```bash
# Your Git Private Match Repo SSH URL
git_url("git@github.com:xxxx/certificates.git")

storage_mode("git")

type("development") # The default type, can be: appstore, adhoc, enterprise or development

# app_identifier(["tools.fastlane.app", "tools.fastlane.app2"])
# username("user@fastlane.tools") # Your Apple Developer Portal username

# For all available options run `fastlane match --help`
# Remove the # at the beginning of the line to enable the other options

# The docs are available on https://docs.fastlane.tools/actions/match
```

translations.```bash\nplatform :ios do\n lane :match:
```bash
platform :ios do
  lane :match_development do |options|
    # Replace with your App Identifier ID
    app_identifier = "li.zhgchg.myApp"

    type = options.fetch(:type, "development")
    isRead = options.fetch(:isRead, true)
    if isRead 
      readonly = true
      force = false
    else
      readonly = false
      force = true

      # App Store Connect API Key is required to manage certificates on Apple's backend
      # Assuming the App Store Connect API .p8 Key is in the ./fastlane/ directory
      app_store_connect_api_key(
        key_id: "XXXXXX",
        issuer_id: "XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX",
        key_filepath: "./fastlane/AuthKey_XXXXXX.p8",
      )
    end

    # Fetch Development certificates for the app_identifier
    match(
      type: type,
      app_identifier: app_identifier, 
      readonly: readonly, # Whether to update/upload cert/profile when needed
      force: force # Whether to forcibly recreate the provisioning profile
    )
  end
end
```

translations.```php\nplatform :ios do \n desc \"Reg:
```php
platform :ios do  
  desc "Register a new device and refresh profiles"
  lane :registerDevice do |options|
    # Replace with your App Identifier ID
    app_identifier = "li.zhgchg.myApp"

    # App Store Connect API Key is required to manage certificates on Apple backend
    # Assume the App Store Connect API .p8 Key is in the ./fastlane/ directory
    app_store_connect_api_key(
      key_id: "XXXXXX",
      issuer_id: "XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX",
      key_filepath: "./fastlane/AuthKey_XXXXXX.p8",
    )
    # Input: UDID and device name
    udid = options[:udid] || UI.input("Enter device UDID:")
    device_name = options[:name] || UI.input("Enter device name:")
    UI.message("📱 Registering device #{device_name} (#{udid})")
    register_device(
      name: device_name,
      udid: udid,
      platform: 'ios'
    )

    # Update Development certificates for app_identifier
    match(
      type: "development",
      app_identifier: app_identifier, 
      readonly: false, # Update/upload cert/profile if needed
      force_for_new_devices: true # Recreate provisioning profile for new devices
    )

    # Update AdHoc certificates for app_identifier
    match(
      type: "adhoc",
      app_identifier: app_identifier, 
      readonly: false, # Update/upload cert/profile if needed
      force_for_new_devices: true # Recreate provisioning profile for new devices
    )
  end
end
```

translations.```yaml\nname: CI - Deploy\n\non:\n pus:
```yaml
name: CI - Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout current repo (Repo A)
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup SSH for Deploy Key
        run: |
          mkdir -p ~/.ssh
          echo "${{ secrets.MATCH_REPO_DEPLOY_PRIVATE_KEY }}" > ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
          chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
          ssh-keyscan github.com >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts

      - name: Test Clone
        run: |
          git clone git@github.com:xxxx/match-certificates.git
# Success!
# .. do deploy job...
```
translations.```yaml\nname: CI - Deploy\non:\n push::
```yaml
name: CI - Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout current repo (Repo A)
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup SSH for Deploy Key
        run: |
          mkdir -p ~/.ssh
          echo "${{ secrets.MATCH_REPO_DEPLOY_PRIVATE_KEY }}" > ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
          chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
          ssh-keyscan github.com >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts  
      
      - name: Write Secret Key to File
        env:
          APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_CONTENT: ${{ secrets.APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_CONTENT }}
        run: |
          # ensure fastlane directory exists
          mkdir -p ./fastlane
      
          # create file path
          APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_PATH=./fastlane/AuthKey_XXXXXX.p8
      
          # write content to file (keep newline)
          echo "$APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_CONTENT" > "$APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_PATH"
      
          # (optional) restrict permissions
          chmod 600 "$APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_PATH"

      - name: Create fastlane keychain
        env:
          KEYCHAIN_NAME: "${{ runner.name }}"
          MATCH_PASSWORD: "${{ secrets.MATCH_PASSWORD }}"
        run: |
          bundle exec fastlane run create_keychain \
            name:"$KEYCHAIN_NAME" \
            password:"$MATCH_PASSWORD" \
            unlock:true \
            timeout:0 \
            lock_when_sleeps:false
      
      - name: Deploy to Firebase
        env:
          MATCH_PASSWORD: "${{ secrets.MATCH_PASSWORD }}"
          KEYCHAIN_NAME: "${{ runner.name }}"
        run: bundle exec fastlane deploy_to_firebase

      # 🔥 This will run regardless of previous success or failure
      - name: Delete fastlane keychain
        if: always()
        env:
          KEYCHAIN_NAME: ${{ runner.name }}
        run: |
          bundle exec fastlane run delete_keychain \
            name:"$KEYCHAIN_NAME"
```

```yaml
name: CI - Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout current repo (Repo A)
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup SSH for Deploy Key
        run: |
          mkdir -p ~/.ssh
          echo "${{ secrets.MATCH_REPO_DEPLOY_PRIVATE_KEY }}" > ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
          chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
          ssh-keyscan github.com >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts  
      
      - name: Write Secret Key to File
        env:
          APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_CONTENT: ${{ secrets.APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_CONTENT }}
        run: |
          # ensure fastlane directory exists
          mkdir -p ./fastlane
      
          # create file path
          APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_PATH=./fastlane/AuthKey_XXXXXX.p8
      
          # write content to file (keep newline)
          echo "$APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_CONTENT" > "$APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_PATH"
      
          # (optional) restrict permissions
          chmod 600 "$APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_PATH"

      - name: Deploy to Firebase
        env:
          MATCH_PASSWORD: "${{ secrets.MATCH_PASSWORD }}"
        run: bundle exec fastlane deploy_to_firebase
```
translations.`keychain_password` 我就沒特別另外設定了，統一用 `MATC: I didn't set a separate `keychain_password`; I just used `MATCH_PASSWORD` uniformly.
translations.login keychain -&gt; All items -&gt; 搜尋: login keychain -> All items -> Search `apple development` -> Delete all Certificates
translations.人員異動後會失去 Match Repo 權限，可以 Revoke 舊的憑證重新產: After personnel changes, access to the Match Repo will be lost. You can revoke the old certificates and regenerate them; others just need to pull again (it’s even smoother if integrated into the make project script).
translations.介紹 Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles: Introduction to the Relationship Between Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles and Using Fastlane Match to Centralize Certificate Issuance Management and Integrate into CI/CD Workflows
translations.代表建立的 Development/Distribution Certifica: Indicates that the created Development/Distribution Certificates have reached the maximum limit.
translations.內容輸入你設定的 Fastlane Match Repo `Passphrase: Enter the `Passphrase` for the Fastlane Match Repo you configured
translations.其他開發者、CI/CD 服務都 **只有讀取** Pull 憑證權限\n: Other developers and CI/CD services have **read-only** permission to pull certificates.
translations.其實 Fastlane 也幫我們考慮到了，可以在 Match 之前先建立一個乾淨: Fastlane actually considers this for us and allows creating a clean Keychain before running Match:
translations.刪除 Keychain 裡的所有舊 Certificates 憑證、Profil: Delete all old Certificates and Profiles in the Keychain
translations.到 [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles]: Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} Devices to verify that the physical device's Identifier is registered.
Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} Identifiers to confirm the Bundle Identifier is registered.
Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} to download the corresponding Profile for use.

Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} and make sure the corresponding Profile has the physical device enabled.

Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} Profiles to confirm that the provisioning profile has the corresponding certificate checked or recreate the profile for use.

Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} to revoke the old Certificate and generate a new one  
(Don't worry, this won't affect the live App; it only impacts development and packaging stages)
Go to [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} to delete unused certificates.
translations.只有負責管理憑證的人可以產生/更新、Push 上傳憑證\n: Only the person responsible for managing certificates can generate/update and push certificates.
translations.可參考前文提到的常見錯誤，多半是有舊的髒憑證，清除所有憑證再重新拉取，應該就可以: You can refer to the common errors mentioned earlier. Most are caused by old, corrupted certificates. Clearing all certificates and re-downloading them should resolve the issue.
translations.同問題 1 步驟，在主要 Repo 新增 `MATCH_PASSWORD` 到: For Step 1 of the same issue, add `MATCH_PASSWORD` to Secrets in the main repo.
translations.同問題 1 步驟，在主要 Repo 新增一個 `APP_STORE_CONNEC: For the same issue 1 step, add an `APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_CONTENT` Secret in the main Repo.
translations.因為 GitHub Actions 不能存檔案，所以只能先存成字串，然後寫入檔案: Because GitHub Actions cannot store files directly, you must first save it as a string and then write it to a file.
translations.團隊大、App 數量多時要寄來寄去的會很麻煩，而且 **每年都要更新一次**\n: When the team is large and there are many apps, sending files back and forth becomes very troublesome, and **they need to be renewed every year**.
translations.在 Apple 生態系中，App 開發是基於憑證與描述檔控管的，跟 Androi: In the Apple ecosystem, app development is based on managing certificates and provisioning profiles, unlike Android where having the .apk file is enough to install and use. Apple enforces strict certificate matching rules; if these are not met, the app cannot be installed or used.
translations.在導入 Match 統一管理憑證之前，每位開發者都會建立自己的 `Develop: Before adopting Match for unified certificate management, each developer creates their own `Development Certificate` and `Development Profile`; if there are 1,000+ developers in the organization, the backend of [Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles, Devices](https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/certificates/list){:target="_blank"} becomes extremely chaotic and unmanageable.
translations.在當初創建 Certificate 的電腦 Keychain 上找到該憑證，匯出: Find the certificate in the Keychain of the computer where the Certificate was originally created, export it as a `.p12` file, and install it on the problematic computer.
translations.執行完畢，不管成功或失敗都會刪除\n: Deleted after execution regardless of success or failure
translations.基於以上問題我們希望能有一個平台幫我們代為管理所有跟憑證有關的事物，所有開發者跟: To address the above issues, we want a platform to manage everything related to certificates on our behalf. All developers and CI/CD services will pull and update data uniformly from this platform. The storage of this platform must be secure, and that is — [Fastlane Match](https://docs.fastlane.tools/actions/match/){:target="_blank"} .
translations.大致介紹完 Fastlane Match 怎麼產生 Push / 拉取 Pull: After a general introduction to how Fastlane Match generates and pulls certificates, next is to explain how to integrate it into the CI/CD process.
translations.如果有外包團隊，只負責開發工作，還是需要把他加入到 Apple Develope: If there is an outsourced team responsible only for development, they still need to be added to the Apple Developer Program portal to generate their own development certificates and profiles.
translations.如果要細分權限可以分兩個 Repos，一個管理 Development 憑證、一: If you want to separate permissions, you can use two Repos: one to manage Development certificates and another to manage Distribution certificates.
translations.常見做法是由一位工程師建立產生 Distribution Certificate: A common practice is for one engineer to create the Distribution Certificate and then export the .p12 file to other developers who need to publish the app or to use it on the CI/CD machines.
translations.建議產生一個 [隨機字串](https://www.random.org/str: It is recommended to generate a [random string](https://www.random.org/strings/?num=1&len=32&digits=on&upperalpha=on&loweralpha=on&unique=on&format=html&rnd=new){:target="_blank"}, then set and record it.
translations.我們以 Git Storage 為例，所有憑證。\n\n: Let's take Git Storage as an example for all certificates.
translations.所有人共用同一張 Development & Distribution Cert: Everyone Shares the Same Development & Distribution Certificate
translations.把憑證結果 ( `.mobileprovision` Profiles, `.p:
Encrypt and upload the three certificate files (`.mobileprovision` Profiles, `.p12` Certificate, `.cer` Certificate) to the Git Repo (or other storage).  
The `.cer` Certificate is also stored separately to verify the certificate's validity.
translations.日後更新或是其他人拉取 Match Repo 憑證都需要輸入這個 字串 解密回原: In future updates or when others pull the Match Repo certificates, this string is required to decrypt back to the original files.
translations.最近到新環境又重新跑了一次，每次都有學習到新的東西，這次聚焦在 iOS Code: Recently, I set up a new environment and went through the process again. Each time, I learn something new. This time, I focused on iOS Codesigning: the relationship between Certificates, Profiles, and Devices, and how I use Fastlane Match to connect certificate management with CI/CD.
translations.有 iOS Distribution/iOS Development Certi: Have an iOS Distribution/iOS Development Certificate **but no corresponding Private Key**
translations.有任何問題及指教歡迎 [與我聯絡](https://www.zhgchg.li/: If you have any questions or feedback, feel free to [contact me](https://www.zhgchg.li/contact){:target="_blank"}.
translations.每個 Runner 同時只會執行一個，因此我們用 Runner Name 當成: Each Runner only executes one task at a time, so we use the Runner Name as the Keychain Name. Each Runner will have its own Keychain.
translations.沒有錯誤就代表 Development Certificate & Profil: No errors mean the Development Certificate & Profile were successfully created, installed, and pushed to the Match Repo.
translations.用文字編輯器打開 `AuthKey_XXXXXX.p8` 複製並貼上內容\n: Open the `AuthKey_XXXXXX.p8` file with a text editor, then copy and paste its content.
translations.目前選擇的 Provisioning profile 描述檔跟當前的 Certi: The currently selected Provisioning Profile does not correspond to the current Certificate.
translations.移出 iCloud 同步目錄即可。\n\n: Simply remove it from the iCloud sync directory.
translations.第一個最常見的問題就是在 CI/CD 上怎麼 Clone Private Mat: The most common issue is how to clone the Private Match Repo project on CI/CD. During local development, we use ssh git clone with our own account's ssh key, so there is no problem. However, CI/CD does not have this key. Although you can use a personal key, it is not secure.
translations.組合 Certificates+Identifiers+Devices 關係。\: Combination of Certificates + Identifiers + Devices Relationships.
translations.統一使用 Fastlane match 指令操作憑證。\n: Use the Fastlane match command uniformly to manage certificates.
translations.綜合以上，如果要在一台乾淨的機器、不登入 Xcode Apple Account: In summary, to build for a physical device (Development Certificate) or perform Archive packaging (Distribution Certificate) on a clean machine without logging into the Xcode Apple Account, **you must have two files**:
translations.註冊完之後其他開發者或 CI/CD 服務再從 Match Repo 拉 Prof: After registration, other developers or CI/CD services pulling the Profile (Development or AdHoc) from the Match Repo will include the new devices.
translations.跟 App Store Connect 做交互 (透過 App Store Co: Interact with App Store Connect (via App Store Connect API or Apple Developer Login Session) to generate or update certificates
translations.跟雲端機器每次都是乾淨全新的不同，如果是用 Self-hosted Runner: Unlike cloud machines that are always clean and new each time, with Self-hosted Runners we can specify `derived_data_path`, `output_directory`, `buildlog_path`, and `reinstall_app` in Fastlane to ensure a clean environment for each run; but since Certificates and Profiles are installed into the system Keychain, how should we handle them?
translations.這個值就是用來加密你的所有 Match Repo 上的檔案的會需要用到的參考 (: This value is used as a reference to encrypt all the files in your Match Repo (passphrase + random salt).
translations.這樣 Match 在拉取憑證的時候就會改存到指定的 Keychain，而非共用的: This way, Match will save the pulled certificates to the specified Keychain instead of the shared login keychain.
translations.這邊還有踩到另一個雷，就是我手賤把專案放在 iCloud 同步目錄下，不知道為何: Here I also ran into another issue because I carelessly placed the project in the iCloud sync folder. For some reason, Fastlane kept showing certificate problems (suspected keychain access issues).
translations.重開 Xcode 應該就正常了\n: Restarting Xcode should fix the issue.
